Key Concepts: One-Step & Two-Step Inequalities

Inequalities use the symbols: < (less than), > (greater than), â‰¤ (less than or equal), â‰¥ (greater than or equal).

Solve inequalities like equations, but flip the inequality sign when multiplying or dividing by a negative number.

Graph solutions on a number line: use an open circle for < or >, and a closed circle for â‰¤ or â‰¥.
